goal: wave16-content-supply: Ship SITE_DESIGN_v2 Wave 16 (content supply — L12 editorial draft ingest + seed content) per apps/backend/src/lanes/editorial-supply/INTERFACE.md and .editorial-orbita/content-brief.md. Wave 16 delivers: (1) L12 backend editorial-supply lane — POST/GET /api/internal/editorial/drafts, approve/reject for ADMIN_EMAILS session; same create-draft contract callable via L11 Bearer on /api/v1/objects/drafts (reuse Wave 12 object store, no parallel DB). (2) Extend L10 compile-draft to include published knowledge/community objects + curated links for newsletter prep (Wave 17), not contributions-only. (3) Idempotent seed path meeting content-brief minimums: >=8 published .org knowledge objects + >=5 .org community highlights (mixed types), align data/curated/org-home.json slugs; label editorial seeds in metadata. (4) Minimal .org admin UI for draft queue (ADMIN_EMAILS only) — thin /editorial page listing pending drafts + approve/reject. (5) Update .editorial-orbita runbooks with exact API paths (no Orbita platform code in this repo; Orbita client doc-only, non-blocking). Shared schemas in packages/shared if needed. Run pnpm turbo build and pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test. Update docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + SESSION_HANDOFF.md. Open ONE draft PR to main. English UI/code.
summary: Jacky's bot: shipping SITE_DESIGN_v2 Wave 16 content supply — L12 editorial draft ingest (POST/GET /api/internal/editorial/drafts + approve/reject for admins, Bearer create-draft parity), compile-draft extended to published knowledge/community objects + curated links, an idempotent .org seed (>=8 knowledge + >=5 community highlights) aligned to curated home slugs, and a thin admin /editorial draft-queue UI on .org
acceptanceCriteria: L12 editorial-supply lane exists at apps/backend/src/lanes/editorial-supply/** and is mounted at /api/internal/editorial: POST /api/internal/editorial/drafts (create draft knowledge/community object — ADMIN_EMAILS session OR L11 Bearer with editorial scope), GET /api/internal/editorial/drafts (admin session list pending), POST /api/internal/editorial/drafts/:id/approve (admin publish via existing submit/lifecycle), POST /api/internal/editorial/drafts/:id/reject (admin archive). It REUSES the Wave 12 object store (apps/backend/src/db/objects.ts) — no parallel drafts table/model. The same create-draft payload contract remains callable via L11 Bearer on the existing /api/v1/objects/drafts route.

L10 compile-draft (apps/backend/src/lanes/agent/compile-draft.ts + agent/index.ts POST /api/internal/agent/compile-draft) now includes PUBLISHED knowledge + community objects and curated links (e.g. /knowledge/<slug>, /community) in its output, not contributions-only — so Wave 17 newsletter prep can pull from real published content.

An idempotent seed path (script under scripts/, runnable via tsx/pnpm) creates >=8 published .org knowledge objects and >=5 .org community highlights of mixed types (>=2 discussion, >=1 community_announcement, >=1 event, >=1 help_request), each labeled in metadata as an editorial seed (e.g. metadata.editorial_seed=true, metadata.seed_wave='wave16'); re-running the seed does not duplicate rows. Seed knowledge slugs are aligned with data/curated/org-home.json (curated slugs resolve to seeded/known content; no dangling references).

A thin .org admin /editorial page (ADMIN_EMAILS only) lists pending editorial drafts and exposes approve/reject actions calling the L12 endpoints; non-admins are gated like the existing /moderation page.

.editorial-orbita runbooks (README.md, orbita-connection.md, runbooks/weekly-seed.md) document the EXACT API paths (POST /api/internal/editorial/drafts and the L11 Bearer /api/v1/objects + /api/v1/objects/drafts path) — Orbita client stays doc-only; no Orbita platform code added to this repo; Wave 16 does not block on Orbita.
`pnpm turbo build` passes for all build targets and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` passes (existing 49 tests stay green + new editorial-supply tests).
docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md updated to mark Wave 16 shipped.
Exactly ONE draft PR opened to main (not merged, not marked ready).

tasks.name: backend-editorial
tasks.scopedGoal:
Implement the Wave 16 BACKEND + DATA slice: the L12 editorial-supply lane, the L10 compile-draft extension, an idempotent .org content seed, any needed shared schemas, and the .editorial-orbita runbook API-path updates. Do NOT touch apps/web-io/** or apps/web-org/** (the admin UI is a separate downstream worker that branches from you).

DELIVERABLES:
(1) L12 LANE — Create apps/backend/src/lanes/editorial-supply/index.ts exporting a Hono router, and mount it in apps/backend/src/index.ts with `app.route('/api/internal/editorial', editorialRouter)`. Endpoints (per apps/backend/src/lanes/editorial-supply/INTERFACE.md):
   - POST /api/internal/editorial/drafts — create a DRAFT knowledge or community object. Accept ADMIN_EMAILS session (resolve via resolveRequester + isAdmin from src/lib/admin.ts) OR an L11 write Bearer (the existing verifyWriteToken path used in lanes/objects/index.ts). Reuse the Wave 12 object store: call saveObjectDraft/createObject from src/db/objects.ts with status 'draft' and stamp metadata.editorial_source so drafts are queryable. Validate community type metadata with normalizeCommunityMetadata/getCommunityTypeFields like lanes/objects/index.ts does. NO parallel drafts table.
   - GET /api/internal/editorial/drafts — ADMIN session only; list pending editorial drafts (status 'draft'/'pending') via listObjectsForRequester (or a small dedicated query) filtered to editorial drafts; return id, objectType, type, site, title, body excerpt, status, createdAt, metadata.
   - POST /api/internal/editorial/drafts/:id/approve — ADMIN session only; publish the draft through the EXISTING submit/lifecycle flow (submitObject and/or updateObjectLifecycle from src/db/objects.ts) so it becomes status 'published' and visible on /knowledge or /community.
   - POST /api/internal/editorial/drafts/:id/reject — ADMIN session only; archive/discard via updateObjectLifecycle (status 'archived' or equivalent).
   The same create-draft payload must remain callable via the existing L11 Bearer route POST /api/v1/objects/drafts (objectsRouter is already mounted at both /api and /api/v1) — do not break that; if you add an 'editorial scope' notion to the bearer, keep the existing objects bearer path working.
(2) COMPILE-DRAFT — Extend apps/backend/src/lanes/agent/compile-draft.ts (compileIssueDraftMarkdown) and its caller in lanes/agent/index.ts so the compiled newsletter draft ALSO includes published knowledge + community objects (query published objects for the site via src/db/objects.ts, e.g. listObjectsForRequester with status 'published') and curated links (e.g. /knowledge/<slug>, /community). Keep the existing contributions sections; ADD sections for published knowledge and community highlights with links. Keep it deterministic (no LLM). Add/adjust newsletter tests accordingly.
(3) SEED — Add an IDEMPOTENT seed script under scripts/ (e.g. scripts/seed-editorial-content.ts, runnable with `pnpm tsx scripts/seed-editorial-content.ts` or wired as a package script) that inserts into the SAME object store (src/db/objects.ts via the backend db) >=8 published .org KNOWLEDGE objects and >=5 published .org COMMUNITY highlights of MIXED types (at least: 2 discussion, 1 community_announcement, 1 event, 1 help_request). Use real, English, brand-appropriate content sourced/condensed from knowledge-base/*.md (there are 10 articles: what-is-ai-transformation, transformation-roadmap, common-pitfalls, ai-patterns-copilots-agents-automation, governance-and-operating-model, measuring-ai-value, use-cases-by-industry, ai-transformation-vs-digital-transformation, glossary, faq). Set site 'org', objectType 'knowledge'/'community', visibility 'public', status 'published', publishedSlug aligned to data/curated/org-home.json articleSlugs/spotlight (what-is-ai-transformation, transformation-roadmap, common-pitfalls, ai-patterns-copilots-agents-automation) so curated references resolve. Label every seeded row metadata.editorial_seed=true and metadata.seed_wave='wave16'. IDEMPOTENT: before insert, check for an existing object with the same site+publishedSlug (or seed key) and skip/update rather than duplicate. If data/curated/org-home.json needs a slug tweak to stay consistent with seeds, make it (you own that file).
(4) SHARED SCHEMAS — Only if needed (e.g. an editorial draft list/approve request schema or editorial bearer scope), add to packages/shared/src/** and regenerate exports; prefer reusing existing wave12-objects/wave13-community schemas.
(5) ORBITA RUNBOOKS — Update .editorial-orbita/README.md, .editorial-orbita/orbita-connection.md, and .editorial-orbita/runbooks/weekly-seed.md to document the EXACT API paths (POST /api/internal/editorial/drafts for admin session; POST /api/v1/objects + POST /api/v1/objects/drafts for L11 Bearer). NO Orbita platform code — doc/runbook only.

TESTS: Add apps/backend/src/lanes/editorial-supply/*.test.ts covering create-draft (admin session AND bearer), admin-only list/approve/reject (401/403 for non-admin), and approve→published transition. Run `pnpm install` (node_modules are usually missing on cloud start), then `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend build` and ensure they pass with no regressions to the existing suite. Run the seed script twice to prove idempotency.

CONSTRAINTS: English code/UI. Backend stays host-agnostic. Do NOT edit packages/shared/src/index.ts agent-entry 'Harvest Hub' text (a backend agent-protocol test asserts on it). Do NOT touch apps/web-io/** or apps/web-org/**.
tasks.brief:
READ FIRST: apps/backend/src/lanes/editorial-supply/INTERFACE.md (authoritative contract), .editorial-orbita/content-brief.md (seed minimums), docs/waves/wave16-content-supply.md (scope + DoD), and load .agents/skills/lane-editorial-supply/SKILL.md + .agents/skills/lane-backend-core/SKILL.md.

KEY EXISTING CODE (reference — reuse, do not reinvent):
- apps/backend/src/index.ts mounts lanes; objectsRouter is mounted at BOTH '/api' and '/api/v1', so POST /api/v1/objects/drafts already exists. Add `app.route('/api/internal/editorial', editorialRouter)` next to `app.route('/api/internal/agent', agentRouter)`.
- apps/backend/src/lanes/objects/index.ts shows the request-resolution pattern: resolveRequester(c) → { site, sessionUser, bearerEmail, bearerOwnerUser, isBearerAuthenticated }, requireAuthenticated(requester), requireResolvedUser(requester), normalizeCommunityMetadata(objectType, type, metadata), getValidationErrorMessage(error). The existing POST /objects/drafts handler is the template for your editorial create-draft.
- apps/backend/src/db/objects.ts: createObject({payload,ownerUserId}), saveObjectDraft({payload,ownerUserId}), submitObject({objectId,publishMode,...}), updateObjectLifecycle({id,status,visibility?,publishedSlug?,metadata?}), listObjectsForRequester({request,context}) (request supports site/objectType/type/status/visibility filters), getObjectByIdForRequester, listModerationQueue, transitionModerationItem. Object fields: objectType ('knowledge'|'community'|...), type, site ('io'|'org'), ownerUserId (nullable), visibility ('public'|'members-only'|'private'), title, subject, body, status (LifecycleStatus enum in packages/shared/src/wave12-objects.ts: includes 'draft' and 'published'), metadata (JSON object), publishedSlug, createdAt, updatedAt.
- apps/backend/src/lib/admin.ts: isAdmin(user) reads ADMIN_EMAILS env (comma list). Admin endpoints follow the lanes/objects moderation gate (401 if no user, 403 if not admin).
- Community types (packages/shared/src/wave12-objects.ts + wave13-community.ts): phase1 discussion, help_request, event, community_announcement; phase2 question, mentorship_request, project_request, collaboration_offer, apprenticeship_opportunity. Use getCommunityTypeFields for metadata validation.
- compile-draft today (apps/backend/src/lanes/agent/compile-draft.ts) only sections contributions from db/newsletter.ts listContributionsSince; you ADD published knowledge/community object sections + curated links.
- Seed against the real backend DB (better-sqlite3 via src/db). Make the seed importable + runnable; idempotency keyed on site+publishedSlug (knowledge) and a stable seed key in metadata (community). data/curated/org-home.json currently references articleSlugs what-is-ai-transformation, transformation-roadmap, common-pitfalls and spotlight ai-patterns-copilots-agents-automation — align seeded knowledge slugs to these.

Backend runtime: backend + combined use tsx (packages/shared exports .ts); backend tsc excludes *.test.ts. Tests: `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test`. Commit on your branch; your handoff MUST list the exact endpoint paths, the seed script command, the seed counts produced (knowledge N / community M by type), idempotency proof (running twice = same counts), and the build+test results. Do NOT open a PR (integration worker does that).
acceptance: apps/backend/src/lanes/editorial-supply/index.ts exists and is mounted at /api/internal/editorial with POST /drafts (admin session OR L11 Bearer), GET /drafts (admin), POST /drafts/:id/approve (admin), POST /drafts/:id/reject (admin); all reuse src/db/objects.ts (no parallel drafts table)
POST /api/v1/objects/drafts (existing Bearer route) still works as the agent create-draft path
compile-draft output includes published knowledge + community objects + curated links (/knowledge/<slug>, /community), not contributions-only

Idempotent seed script under scripts/ creates >=8 published .org knowledge + >=5 mixed-type published .org community highlights, labeled metadata.editorial_seed=true/seed_wave='wave16', with knowledge slugs aligned to data/curated/org-home.json; re-running does not duplicate
.editorial-orbita/README.md + orbita-connection.md + runbooks/weekly-seed.md document the exact create-draft API paths; no Orbita platform code added

New apps/backend/src/lanes/editorial-supply/*.test.ts pass; `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend build` pass with no regressions; no files under apps/web-* changed
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `pnpm install` if node_modules missing.
## Automated
- `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` (existing suite + new editorial tests) and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend build` must pass.
- Run the seed script twice; assert knowledge>=8 and community>=5 (mixed types) and that the second run yields identical counts (idempotent).
- Confirm `app.route('/api/internal/editorial', ...)` is mounted in apps/backend/src/index.ts and POST /api/v1/objects/drafts still resolves.
## Manual / code inspection
- create draft (admin session) → GET list shows it → approve → object status 'published' and returned by listObjectsForRequester(status 'published'); reject → archived. Bearer create-draft via /api/v1/objects/drafts works.
- compile-draft output markdown contains a published-knowledge section and community highlights with /knowledge//community links.
## Gotchas
- Reuse Wave 12 object store; no parallel DB. Do NOT edit packages/shared/src/index.ts (agent-protocol test asserts on its text). English only. Do not touch apps/web-*.

tasks.name: web-org-editorial
tasks.scopedGoal:
Implement the Wave 16 ADMIN UI slice on ai-transformation.org in apps/web-org ONLY, consuming the L12 editorial endpoints delivered on your starting branch (orch/wave16-content-supply/backend-editorial). Add a THIN admin /editorial page (ADMIN_EMAILS only) that lists pending editorial drafts and lets an admin approve or reject each one.

DELIVERABLES:
- apps/web-org/app/editorial/page.tsx + a client component (e.g. apps/web-org/components/editorial-queue.tsx) that: fetches GET /api/internal/editorial/drafts (credentialed), renders each pending draft (objectType, type, site, title, body excerpt, created date), and provides Approve (POST /api/internal/editorial/drafts/:id/approve) and Reject (POST /api/internal/editorial/drafts/:id/reject) buttons that refresh the list on success.
- Gate it to admins EXACTLY like the existing apps/web-org/app/moderation/page.tsx + apps/web-org/components/moderation-panel.tsx pattern (server/admin check + a not-authorized state for non-admins). Mirror that page's data-fetching, auth-gating, loading/empty/error states, and editorial styling — keep it thin and consistent, no new design system.
- English-only UI; light default + dark toggle; load .agents/skills/editorial-ui/SKILL.md + .agents/skills/ux-copy/SKILL.md before writing copy.

Do NOT modify backend code, packages, data, or other apps — only apps/web-org/**. The endpoints already exist on your starting branch; if a response field you need is missing, prefer adapting the UI to what GET /drafts returns rather than editing the backend (note any gap in your handoff). Run `pnpm install` first if node_modules missing, then ensure `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org build` and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org typecheck` pass. Do NOT open a PR.
tasks.brief:
READ FIRST: apps/backend/src/lanes/editorial-supply/INTERFACE.md and the L12 router on your starting branch (apps/backend/src/lanes/editorial-supply/index.ts) to learn the exact GET /drafts response shape and the approve/reject paths. Load .agents/skills/lane-web-org/SKILL.md + editorial-ui + ux-copy.

CLOSEST PATTERN TO COPY: apps/web-org/app/moderation/page.tsx (17 lines, thin) + apps/web-org/components/moderation-panel.tsx (~258 lines) — the existing admin moderation queue. Your /editorial page should be the editorial-draft analogue: same admin gate, same fetch-list-then-act-and-refresh shape, same visual language. Reuse existing API client helpers / fetch conventions used by moderation-panel (credentialed requests to the combined /api proxy). Keep the page minimal — a list of pending drafts with Approve/Reject; no rich editing.

This is frontend-only. The combined proxy routes /api/* to the backend, so the admin session cookie flows through. Capture a screenshot of the /editorial queue (with at least one pending draft and the approve/reject controls) and reference the artifact path in your handoff. Your handoff MUST state the route added, the component(s), and the build/typecheck results. node_modules are usually missing on cloud start — run `pnpm install` first.
acceptance: apps/web-org/app/editorial/page.tsx renders an ADMIN_EMAILS-gated draft queue (non-admins see a not-authorized state, mirroring /moderation)
The queue lists pending editorial drafts from GET /api/internal/editorial/drafts and provides working Approve + Reject buttons calling the L12 approve/reject endpoints, refreshing on success
Editorial look + English copy consistent with the existing /moderation admin UI; light default + dark toggle preserved
`pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org build` + typecheck pass; only apps/web-org/** changed
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `pnpm install` if node_modules missing, then `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/web-org build` + typecheck.
## Automated
- Build + typecheck must pass; confirm only apps/web-org/** changed.
## Manual / code inspection
- /editorial mounts an admin-gated page mirroring /moderation; it fetches GET /api/internal/editorial/drafts and the Approve/Reject buttons POST to the correct :id/approve and :id/reject paths and refresh the list.
## Gotchas
- Frontend-only; do not edit backend/packages/data. English-only, light default + dark toggle. Reuse the moderation page pattern; keep it thin.

tasks.name: integrate-wave16
tasks.scopedGoal:
Produce the single integrated Wave 16 deliverable branch and open ONE draft PR to main. You start from orch/wave16-content-supply/web-org-editorial, which already contains the backend-editorial commits as its ancestor (linear chain) — so NO cross-branch merge is normally required. Verify the branch is complete and ship it.

STEPS:
1. `pnpm install`, then run `pnpm turbo build` (must pass for ALL build targets: web-io, web-org, backend, combined, shared, content) and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` (must pass — existing 49 + new editorial tests). Fix any integration-only breakage (import drift, type mismatches in how web-org consumes the editorial endpoints). If your starting branch is somehow missing backend or web-org commits, merge the missing branch (orch/wave16-content-supply/backend-editorial and/or orch/wave16-content-supply/web-org-editorial) preserving both parents, then rebuild.
2. Run the idempotent seed script from backend-editorial and confirm it produces >=8 published .org knowledge + >=5 mixed-type published .org community objects, and that a second run does not duplicate (record both runs' counts).
3. Update docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md and docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md to mark Wave 16 shipped: L12 editorial-supply lane (POST/GET /api/internal/editorial/drafts + approve/reject for ADMIN_EMAILS, Bearer create-draft parity on /api/v1/objects/drafts), compile-draft extended to published knowledge/community objects + curated links, idempotent .org seed (>=8 knowledge + >=5 community highlights) aligned to curated home slugs, thin admin /editorial draft-queue UI on .org, and .editorial-orbita runbooks updated with exact API paths (Orbita doc-only). Note build + backend test results and that Wave 17 (newsletter pilot) is next.
4. Commit, push exactly your branch, and open a DRAFT PR to main titled 'Wave 16: SITE_DESIGN_v2 content supply (L12 editorial draft ingest + seed)' whose body summarizes each deliverable (L12 lane + endpoints, compile-draft extension, seed counts + idempotency, admin /editorial UI, Orbita runbook paths), lists `pnpm turbo build` + backend test results, and explicitly lists anything deferred with reason. Do NOT merge or mark ready. Check for .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md and use it if present.

English docs/code. Do NOT edit packages/shared/src/index.ts (backend agent-protocol test asserts on its text).
tasks.brief: This is an integration + docs + PR task (the planner does not code, so this worker finalizes). The task tree is LINEAR (backend-editorial → web-org-editorial → integrate), so your starting branch should already contain everything; your main job is the full-repo build/test, the seed-count proof, the two doc updates, and the draft PR. Read the upstream handoffs (pasted into your prompt) for the exact endpoint paths, seed script command, and any deviations/deferred items, and reflect them accurately in the docs + PR body. zbpack on Zeabur must list every deploy package; you are NOT changing deploy config, just verifying the monorepo builds. node_modules are usually missing on cloud start — run `pnpm install` first. Keep merge history with both parents if any merge is needed.
acceptance: Integrated branch contains the L12 backend lane, compile-draft extension, idempotent seed, and the admin /editorial UI (linear chain; both parents preserved if any merge was needed)
`pnpm turbo build` passes for all build targets and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` passes with no regressions
Seed script run on the integrated branch yields >=8 published .org knowledge + >=5 mixed-type published .org community objects; a second run is idempotent (recorded in handoff)
docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md updated to mark Wave 16 shipped
Exactly ONE DRAFT PR opened to main (not merged, not marked ready) summarizing all deliverables + build/test/seed results + any deferrals
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `pnpm install` on the integrated branch.
## Automated
- `pnpm turbo build` (all targets) and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` must pass.
- Seed script run twice: knowledge>=8, community>=5 mixed, identical counts on second run.
## Manual / code inspection
- Spot-check the integrated branch has the L12 lane mounted, compile-draft published-content sections, the seed, and the /editorial admin UI. Confirm exactly one DRAFT PR to main exists.
## Gotchas
- Linear chain — usually no merge needed. Do NOT touch packages/shared/src/index.ts. Do not mark the PR ready or merge it.

tasks.name: verify-wave16
tasks.scopedGoal: Verify the integrated Wave 16 branch against the planner acceptanceCriteria and docs/waves/wave16-content-supply.md Definition of Done. Run `pnpm install`, `pnpm turbo build` (confirm all build targets pass), and `pnpm --filter @ai-transformation/backend test` (confirm pass, no regressions, including the new editorial-supply tests). Then confirm with execution + code-inspection evidence that each deliverable is implemented: (1) L12 lane mounted at /api/internal/editorial with POST/GET /drafts + approve/reject, admin-gated, with L11 Bearer create-draft parity on /api/v1/objects/drafts, reusing the Wave 12 object store (no parallel drafts table); (2) compile-draft output includes published knowledge + community objects + curated links (/knowledge/<slug>, /community), not contributions-only; (3) the idempotent seed script produces >=8 published .org knowledge + >=5 mixed-type (>=2 discussion, >=1 announcement, >=1 event, >=1 help_request) published .org community highlights labeled metadata.editorial_seed/seed_wave, with knowledge slugs aligned to data/curated/org-home.json, and re-running does not duplicate (RUN IT TWICE and report both counts); (4) the .org /editorial admin page is ADMIN_EMAILS-gated and lists drafts with working approve/reject; (5) .editorial-orbita README/orbita-connection/weekly-seed document the exact API paths with no Orbita platform code added; (6) docs/CURRENT_STATUS.md + docs/SESSION_HANDOFF.md mark Wave 16 shipped; (7) exactly ONE draft PR to main exists (not merged/ready). Report execution evidence per criterion; do not pattern-match. If the env blocks a live UI/API check, run build + backend tests + the seed twice + code/route inspection (lane mount, compile-draft sections, seed idempotency key, admin gate on /editorial) and mark unit-test-verified or verifier-blocked honestly. Note any criterion that is missing or only partially done as a finding.
acceptance: Verification section includes execution evidence (pnpm turbo build output, backend test output, seed-run-twice counts, and per-deliverable code/route inspection) for every planner acceptance criterion and each Wave 16 deliverable
